author: priya.nair
text: Load test just flagged stale pricing on ~8% of checkout requests. Digging in now.

author: priya.nair
text: Found it — each payment-service pod has its own in-memory cache, and price-update invalidation events only reach the pod that received the webhook, not all replicas.

author: marcus.lee
text: Given BF is in 3 weeks I'd rather we move to a shared cache than try to bolt-on cross-pod invalidation. We already run Redis for sessions, seems like the obvious choice.

author: priya.nair
text: Agreed, opening PAY-102 and will have a PR up today.

author: wei.zhang
text: Posted RFC-004 for the orders datastore decision — Postgres vs Mongo. Main question is whether we need transactional guarantees across order + line items + payment record.

author: amara.okafor
text: Catalog service already uses Mongo, so there's an infra argument for consistency there too, but I think orders is fundamentally relational data. +1 Postgres.

author: wei.zhang
text: Consensus seems to be Postgres given the transactional requirement outweighs the schema-flexibility argument. Closing out RFC-004, will implement in ORD-58.

author: amara.okafor
text: Seeing a credential-stuffing pattern against /auth/login from a rotating set of IPs — hundreds of attempts per minute across many accounts.

author: marcus.lee
text: Let's get a rate limit on login attempts asap, we can tune the exact threshold later.

author: amara.okafor
text: PR #190 up with a 5/15min per-account limit, based on OWASP's default since we don't have our own traffic baseline yet.

author: marcus.lee
text: Left a comment on the PR — worried about shared office IPs tripping this, but agree we should ship the conservative default now given the active incident.

author: tom.harrison
text: Billing timeouts spiking. Looks like it's waiting on calls to notifications-service, which is also degraded right now (unrelated DB issue on their side).

author: tom.harrison
text: Confirmed — billing's call to notifications has no timeout, so it just piles up threads waiting. Need a circuit breaker so this can't cascade again.

author: wei.zhang
text: +1, and we should alert when it trips so we know we're silently skipping notifications rather than finding out from a customer complaint.

author: tom.harrison
text: Kicking off the monolith split per RFC-007. Starting with billing since it has the narrowest dependency surface of anything we profiled.

author: sofia.moretti
text: Makes sense, notifications and reporting both have way more cross-module calls, would be riskier to extract first.
